senses.definition: a person from Scotland or of Scottish descent
examples: The crowd cheered as the Scot walked onto the stage.
She wears a tartan scarf to honor her Scottish Scot heritage.

senses.definition: to recover or fetch something in dialectal English; to scotch a plan (see other sense) but primary verb form is 'to scotch' meaning to thwart or quash
examples: He scotched the rumor by presenting evidence.
They attempted to scotch the project, but it continued anyway.

senses.definition: a strong alcoholic drink made in Scotland from malted barley
examples: He enjoyed a glass of Scotch after dinner.
The distillery produced a smooth Scotch aged for twelve years.

senses.definition: a fabric or material from Scotland; or a term used for a strip or patch in tailoring (less common)
examples: He wore a kilt with a tartan of deep blue Scotch.
The pattern resembles traditional Scotch cloth.

senses.definition: to irritate or injure by rubbing; or to hamper by obstacles (less common regional use)
examples: The rough wool began to scotch his skin.
Heat scotched his plans temporarily.

senses.definition: past tense of scotch; to thwart, prevent, or decisively stop something
examples: The last-minute error scotched the deal.
Weather conditions scotched the expedition.

senses.definition: a person who scotches or thwarts; a tool or device used to scotch (rare)
examples: The scotcher of rumors faced scrutiny.
In old texts, a scotcher might refer to someone who stops plans.

senses.definition: place or business dealing with Scotch whisky or scotch products; a shop or shop-like establishment selling such items
examples: They visited a scotchery for rare bottles.
The scotchery specialized in aged single malts.

senses.definition: multiple kinds or bottles of Scotch whisky
examples: The collection includes several fine scotches.
They tasted three scotches from different regions.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of scotch (to thwart or stop)
examples: The plans scotches the competition from proceeding.
New regulations scotches the project temporarily.

senses.definition: the process or act of scotching; in some contexts, adaptation of Scotch-related terminology into English usage
examples: The scotchification of the product line included new labeling.
Scholars discuss the scotchification of regional terms.

senses.definition: to make something Scotch or to apply scotch rules or characteristics; to scotch a plan or declaration (informal/colloquial)
examples: They decided to scotchify the rumor with official statements.
The team scotchified their strategy to prevent leaks.

senses.definition: the quality of being Scotch or characteristic of Scotch whisky or culture; or a rough or brash quality
examples: The scotchiness of the flavor stood out.
Her scotchiness in humor can be surprising.

senses.definition: the act of scotching; the action of thwarting or stopping
examples: Scotching of the plan took effort.
His scotching of the rumor was swift.

senses.definition: a man from Scotland or of Scottish descent
examples: A proud scotchman spoke about his homeland.
The scotchman shared traditional songs.

senses.definition: plural of scotchman; men from Scotland
examples: The scotchmen gathered at the pub.
Several scotchmen shared stories of the Highlands.

senses.definition: the quality of being Scottish or having Scottish characteristics
examples: The scotchness of the fabric gave it a rustic look.
Her scotchness was evident in her accent and mannerisms.

senses.definition: a woman from Scotland or of Scottish descent
examples: The scotchwoman told stories of Edinburgh.
A scotchwoman contributed her cultural traditions.

senses.definition: resembling or characteristic of Scotch whisky; syrupy or sweet in flavor or appearance
examples: The glaze had a scotchy sweetness.
A scotchy aroma filled the room.

senses.definition: a small duck or a fast electric scooter depending on context
examples: The diver spotted a scoter on the lake.
He rode a scoter to commute around town.

senses.definition: plural of scoter; either ducks of the genus Melanitta or scooters
examples: They observed several scoters on the bay.
Urban transport options include electric scoters.

senses.definition: relating to or resembling Scotian or Scotland-related terms; note: this term appears to be rare or non-standard; included for completeness
examples: The scoterythrous dialect features unique intonation.

senses.definition: a region in the south of Eastern Canada; or the broad region of Scotland's name used in various contexts
examples: Scotia Bank in Canada dominates the market.
Nova Scotia is a province in Canada.

senses.definition: of or relating to Scotland or Scots; Scottish in character
examples: Scottish traditions are strong in this town.
The film uses a very scotic backdrop.
